definition: An ancient Mesopotamian temple structure built as a series of rectangular tiers or stepped platforms stacked on top of each other, with a shrine or temple at the top.
translation: 古代美索不達米亞的階梯式神廟；呈層級遞減的塔形建築，由多個矩形平台堆疊而成，頂部設有神殿
example: The Great Ziggurat of Ur, built around 2100 BCE, is one of the most well-preserved examples of ancient Mesopotamian architecture.
